product_description: The Gritty Jackson Dry Sunburns stands out with its gradient back pocket embroidery, shifting from deep orange to pale yellow, with our own Nudie Jeans Orange at the center. Paired with a special graphic Jacron patch, it subtly plays with our identity and iconography. Beneath it all lies a proprietary 14.2 oz denim, developed exclusively for Nudie Jeans. Woven with Regenerative Organic Cotton, it revives the raw, textured character of a pair of ‘90s jeans we once stumbled upon. Though undeniably "modern," these jeans are rooted in a transitional era—a time when vintage ring-spun textures resurfaced, and select denim mills strayed from pure efficiency in pursuit of true vintage aesthetics. A defining moment—just before Nudie Jeans was born. The medium-deep pure indigo warp lays the foundation for a break-in journey with options—left raw, it builds stark contrasts; washed early, it fades with a slow, balanced grace. Stiff and substantial, it forms deep creases and sharp wear marks, evolving over time as the warp streakiness fully emerges. The result? A layered interplay of slub, clean twill lines, and subtle marbling—a denim built to become tomorrow’s vintage. Regenerative organic cotton meets all the standards of organic cotton, avoiding synthetic chemicals and GMOs, but goes further by restoring ecosystems, improving soil health, and boosting biodiversity. It considers the whole system – land, water, people, and long-term resilience.

denim_cat: 16 oz. rigid Italian selvedge denim from Candiani
product_description: Nudie Jeans Selvage LAB is a special collection of limited-edition jeans. Limited to 107 pieces, the Tuff Tony LAB 35 is a denim masterpiece made from premium Italian selvedge denim, woven by Europe’s most esteemed denim mill, Candiani. This exclusive pair is the result of both masterful denim craftsmanship and an intensive, artisanal laundry process led by a specialist team in our denim lab. Every detail - from yarns to finished jeans - has been shaped by highly skilled hands through a time-consuming process that brings out a truly authentic, worn-in character. At the heart of this creation is a Nudie Jeans legend — the very craftsman who, over a decade ago, gave us the iconic Stonemason Replica. His dedication and expertise not only defined that era but also earned him the role of laboratory manager. Now, as he approached retirement, he returned one final time to lead this project - rolling up his sleeves once more to lend his masterful touch. Crafted from 16 oz. pure indigo selvedge denim with a rich ring-spun texture, these jeans showcase both weaving and washing at their finest. The irregular vertical slub and subtle crosshatch weave express a true vintage soul, while the deep indigo warp delivers vibrant highlights and extraordinary depth. Authentic wear marks, carefully placed repairs, and subtle tinting capture the unmistakable aged Nudie Jeans aesthetic.

product_description: Loose fit jeans with a straight leg made from a 14.15 oz. dry denim greatness that resembles an old, '50s style, denim. True to history it is constructed of ring-spun warp and weft yarns with irregular thickness, as was often the case back in the day on workwear fabrics. Also true to its heritage is the dark, but not blackish, indigo warp that is dyed in pure indigo. With wear and wash, the yarns will interact, and you'll reap the rewards that heavier ring-spun denim produces — evident slubs, a pleasing crosshatch as well as a marbling -effect. It's the irregular yarns in both warp and weft that render the crosshatch effect, and the built-in tension formed by the yarns that produce the marble effect after washing. As this denim is dyed in pure indigo, it will age to a clean, blue redcast tone. The greatest part of these jeans is not what they look like new, but rather the complete opposite - how they will look tomorrow, and the many days to come. Enjoy ageing, it is worth the trouble.

product_description: Loose-fit jeans with a high waist and wide leg. This fabric was developed exclusively for Nudie Jeans back in 2022. Crafted and brought to life by the Japanese denim powerhouse Kurabo Denim. At 13.5 oz. This mid-weight denim hits a sweet spot between weight and robust feel. The fabric carries a hand that is more textured than smooth while still offering great comfort. While it's barely visible in the dry state, this denim features a green cast that'll show its full potential only when faded and broken in. The fabric composition features long slub, ring-spun yarns for both warp and weft, which creates an evident crosshatch texture. A revival of a great fabric. Limited to 150 pieces. Breaking in dry denim is a metamorphosis. The interplay between the garment and the user is a spectacular transformation happening slowly in front of your eyes. Over time, with wearing and washing, the indigo fades, exposing the fabric's texture, and eventually unveiling an intricate span of hues. This creates a unique, one-off version of the jeans.

product_description: Pitch black jeans made in dry comfort stretch denim that stays black. Our Everblack fabric is made to maintain its color for as long as possible, withstanding 40, or more washes without any significant loss of color. If you are looking for a pair of pitch-black jeans that do not fade this is the way to go. Keen on knowing what differentiates Everblack from other black denim? Normally, when denim is dyed part of the dye will absorb in the cotton fibers while the rest remains bound only to the surface of the yarns. This is what gives denim its aging property that in many cases is sought after. However, when it comes to black jeans this is not always the case which is why we developed the Everblack denim. The technical difference between Everblack and regular black denim is that it utilizes a method and type of dyestuff that allows improved absorption as well as a stronger fixation. This results in denim with a significantly greater colorfastness, which does not fade with wearing and washing.

wash_state: in an impeccably crafted wash, s more than just a wash
product_description: A mid waist regular-fit jean with a flared leg, in an impeccably crafted wash. But it's more than just a wash—it's a story. Hannes Levin, son of founders Maria and Joakim, and a stand-up guy who regularly lends a helping hand to us, once strolled in wearing a pair of worn-in Flare Glenns. Those Glenns blew our minds—literally, we stood there in silence, all thinking the same thing: these are a piece of Nudie Jeans history, showcasing exactly why we do what we do. Hannes, sensing our awe, likely broke the somewhat odd silence with a humble "What's up?" and, well, he had to part with his jeans for a while. Fast forward, and what you have before you is a meticulous recreation of Hannes' iconic jeans, featuring defined vertical slubbiness and a distinct crosshatch texture. The denim's hue leans towards a greenish-blue, while the abrasions tell their own story with a warmly dusty charm. Hannes (and Maria and Joakim), we owe you big time for not just the style, but for the legacy you've bestowed upon us.

denim_cat: 13.5 oz. Japanese comfort stretch selvedge denim
product_description: Our slim-fit champ Lean Dean in a new spectacular deep-tone, vintage-like, comfort stretch selvedge denim from Kurabo, Japan. This unique selvedge denim emerged from a close collaboration with Kurabo, aiming to blend the rustic charm of vintage denim with modern comfort stretch. Crafted to exude the aura of a '50s vintage selvedge denim, we opted for a rigid-like construction that accentuates the weft. Notably, the chosen warp and weft yarns possess a distinctive slubbiness, offering the dry denim an irregular appearance that enhances its rustic allure, and which will become more pronounced as the denim ages. The warp yarns, dyed with pure indigo, underwent an additional dip compared to Kurabo's regular deep indigo tone. By elevating the standard 8 dips of pure indigo to 9, we deepened the indigo hue, enhancing the denim's aesthetic quality and aging property. To intensify the vintage aesthetics, we deliberately refrained from fully finishing the denim by modern standards. By bypassing the singeing process that typically removes protruding fibers, the Apex showcases a slightly 'hairy' appearance, akin to vintage deadstock denim. Our deliberate choice of a deep navy color for the selvedge contrast yarn ensures that the Apex stands out within our selvedge range. As the denim ages, this contrast will become even more striking, adding a unique edge to its evolving character We're honored to collaborate with Kurabo and introduce Apex. We're very excited about the rustic charm it brings our selvedge range and hope you appreciate it as much as we do. We recommend a minimum of 6 months of daily wear before the first wash, although longer wear enhances the unique aging process. While it may seem lengthy, it's a suggestion; your jeans will acquire character with love and wear.

product_description: Pitch black jeans made in power stretch denim that stays black. Our Everblack fabric is made to maintain its color for as long as possible, withstanding 40, or more washes without any significant loss of color. To optimize the denim for the fit, these jeans have been rinsed, making the jeans stretchy and soft. If you are looking for a pair of pitch-black jeans that do not fade this is the way to go. Keen on knowing what differentiates Everblack from other black denim? Normally, when denim is dyed part of the dye will absorb in the cotton fibers while the rest remains bound only to the surface of the yarns. This is what gives denim its aging property that in many cases is sought after. However, when it comes to black jeans this is not always the case which is why we developed the Everblack denim. The technical difference between Everblack and regular black denim is that it utilizes a method and type of dyestuff that allows improved absorption as well as a stronger fixation. This results in denim with a significantly greater colorfastness, which does not fade with wearing and washing.

product_description: Nudie Jeans Selvage LAB is a special collection of limited-edition jeans. The Steady Eddie II LAB 34 is a denim masterpiece made from premium Italian selvedge denim, woven by Europe’s most esteemed denim mill, Candiani. This exclusive pair is the result of both masterful denim craftsmanship and an intensive, artisanal laundry process led by a specialist team in our denim lab. Every detail - from yarns to finished jeans - has been shaped by highly skilled hands through a time-consuming process that brings out a truly authentic, worn-in character. At the heart of this creation is a Nudie Jeans legend - the very craftsman who, over a decade ago, gave us the iconic Stonemason Replica. His dedication and expertise not only defined that era but also earned him the role of laboratory manager. Now, as he approaches retirement, he returned one final time to lead this project - rolling up his sleeves once more to lend his masterful touch. Crafted from 16 oz. pure indigo selvedge denim with a rich ring-spun texture, these jeans showcase both weaving and washing at their finest. The irregular vertical slub and subtle crosshatch weave express a true vintage soul, while the deep indigo warp delivers vibrant highlights and extraordinary depth. Authentic wear marks, carefully placed repairs, and subtle tinting capture the unmistakable aged Nudie Jeans aesthetic.

product_description: The Rad Rufus Dry Sunburns is a special iteration of the Sunburns concept, crafted from Kaihara Mills rigid denim. Staying true to the Sunburns idea, Rufus stands out with gradient back pocket embroidery, shifting from deep orange to pale yellow, with our signature Nudie Jeans Orange at the center. A specially designed graphic Jacron patch adds a subtle nod to our identity and iconography. Beneath it all lies a 14.75 oz denim marvel from the legendary Kaihara Mills in Japan. Developed exclusively for Nudie Jeans, it is woven with organic cotton and showcases a raw interplay of slubby yarns in both warp and weft, creating a rustic, textured character that is as captivating to the eye as it is to the touch. Dyed by the indigo masters at Kaihara, the medium-dark, pure indigo warp lays the foundation for a break-in journey with choices: left raw, it develops stark contrasts; washed early, it fades with a slow, balanced grace. Stiff and substantial, the denim forms deep creases and sharp wear marks, evolving over time as warp and weft slub interact in a display of rustic yet refined character. The result is a rich interplay of slub, bold twill lines, and subtle marbling—a denim built to become tomorrow’s vintage.

product_description: We are thrilled to introduce our wide-leg, loose-fit, Tuff Tony in the extraordinary Dry Ace Selvage. This remarkable fabric, weighing 14.2 oz showcase a rustic texture and a deep, redcast indigo hue, is a true masterpiece. Crafted by the renowned Shinya Mills in Okayama, Japan, it exemplifies the art of perfecting selvedge denim imperfections. The story behind Dry Ace Selvage is as fascinating as the fabric itself. It all began with a vintage, unbranded pair of jeans believed to date back to the 1950s, discovered in a thrift store somewhere in the USA. These jeans displayed wear and significant fading, and most importantly an incredible texture. They made their way to Japan, where denim from the jeans was meticulously reverse-engineered in exhaustive detail. The art of reverse engineering denim is a hallmark of the artisanal Japanese denim industry. Dry Ace Selvage is a remarkable example of this craft, as well as a testament to the unique collaborative approach sometimes employed for premium Japanese denim. The rather extreme slub yarns are spun by Yamachu Mengyo Co. Ltd. in Osaka. The warp yarns undergo an impressive 20 dips in pure indigo dye, resulting in a deeply saturated surface with a notably substantial undyed core, courtesy of Kaihara Co. Ltd. in Hiroshima. Finally, the fabric is masterfully woven under low tension on vintage shuttle looms by Shinya Mills in Okayama. The guiding principle here is – do what you do best – and the final product beautifully reflects the craftsmanship at each stage of the denim's creation. Ace's distinctive character arises from its thick and highly irregular yarns, generous use of indigo, and low-tension weaving. These factors contribute to its appealingly rugged aging property. Over time, the dye rapidly abrades from the surface of the yarns, emphasizing their slub, particularly in the vertical direction. When, or if, the jeans are washed, the yarns slightly rearrange, reducing the vertical slub and transitioning to a rustic crosshatch texture with a coarse salt 'n pepper effect. Notably, the heavily saturated surface of the indigo yarns entails an extraordinary evolution. Washing initially causes the dye to bleed onto the white weft, increasing the depth of color. With successive washes, the indigo gradually fades into brighter brilliant blue hues while deeper tones are preserved at seams and folds. Due to its unparalleled aging property, Ace unquestionably stands as one of the finest Japanese selvedge denims we have ever encountered. We are therefore very proud to offer it in Tuff Tony.

product_description: Loose-fit jeans with a high waist and wide leg. This fabric was developed exclusively for Nudie Jeans back in 2022. Crafted and brought to life by the Japanese denim powerhouse Kurabo Denim. At 13.5 oz. This mid-weight denim hits a sweet spot between weight and robust feel. The fabric carries a hand that is more textured than smooth while still offering great comfort. While it's barely visible in the dry state, this denim features a green cast that'll show its full potential only when faded and broken in. The fabric composition features long slub, ring-spun yarns for both warp and weft, which creates an evident crosshatch texture. A revival of a great fabric. Limited to 150 pieces. Breaking in dry denim is a metamorphosis. The interplay between the garment and the user is a spectacular transformation happening slowly in front of your eyes. Over time, with wearing and washing, the indigo fades, exposing the fabric's texture, and eventually unveiling an intricate span of hues. This creates a unique, one-off version of the jeans.

denim_cat: 14.5 oz. Japanese rigid selvedge denim from Kurabo
product_description: Our high-waist, regular fit Rad Rufus crafted from our all-time favorite Japanese black selvedge denim. This 14.5 oz. organic selvedge denim boasts a tight weave density and generous starch application, ensuring remarkable rigidity. But what truly distinguishes it is its exceptional aging property, a testament to Kurabo Mills' blend of heritage and innovative denim craftsmanship. This denim stands out due to its unique construction: the warp, visible predominantly on the fabric's face, is yarn-dyed black, allowing it to gradually fade with wear and washes. Conversely, the weft, visible on predominantly on the fabric's back, is dyed with a 'stay black' dye, retaining its deep black hue without fading. Although this might seem unconventional for fading denim, it yields intriguing contrasts, emphasizing the warp-weft intersections. This clever construction promises more pronounced contrasting fades compared to regular dry black denim. Beyond its unique yarn combination, the denim's tight weave, stiff texture, pronounced twill lines and slightly coarse yarns create an exquisite surface structure and marbling effects with wear and washes. For optimal results, we recommend wearing our dry denim regularly for at least six months before the first wash. While entirely optional, this period encourages striking fades.

denim_cat: 13.5 oz. Stretch selvage denim from Bossa, Turkiye
product_description: Slim-fit jeans with a mid waist and straight leg made from 13.5 oz Turkish selvage denim with clear, diagonal, twill lines, a slight warp yarn slub, and a deep, dark, indigo hue. Where wearing exposes the denim for abrasion, a flat surface with clear twill lines will be rendered. On the other hand, the unabraded areas will pucker and render a nice grainy, salt 'n pepper surface. This contrast between surface structures is one of the fabric's greatest traits as it will render wear-marks with a rich variation. The indigo will fade towards a blue hue with a slight greenish-yellowcast. Thanks to the dark indigo hue and the denim structure, wearing and washing will render highlighting wear-marks that contrast deep dark depots of indigo. As with all dry denim, we recommend at least 6 months of daily wear before the first wash. This is, of course, only a recommendation. Washing earlier and more frequently also renders great results, but make sure to either hand wash or use a gentle washing machine cycle. As long as you take care of your jeans, they will, in turn, reward you with an evolving patina that very well might render the best looking washed jeans you will ever have.

product_description: These limited edition jeans come with a story—one made possible by our Re:Use program. Let’s rewind to 2017. At our Nudie Jeans Repair Shop in Gothenburg, an unidentified customer dropped off an impressively worn-in pair of selvedge jeans in exchange for a discount on a new pair. Nothing unusual there—we’ve worked with selvedge denim since 2001, and exchanges like this are common, especially in our hometown. But when the repair team got to work, something about these jeans sparked curiosity. They were cut in an old fit, made from a fabric different from our long-standing Kaihara Dry Selvage. The fit was wide, the denim coarser, and the rivets, buttons, and labels were clearly old—really old (for a pair of Nudie Jeans). The style label? Completely washed out. Word spread quickly to HQ. The mystery deepened. Based on the clues, we suspected it was a 2005 selvedge made in Loose Frank—a rare piece. Or was it maybe just a sample? Captivated by their beauty, we pulled out old product records, combed through collection books, and even booted up our long-retired internal product system—just to follow every possible thread. We emailed mills, compared notes, searched for any match. Still, we couldn’t trace the fabric. The origin of the fabric remained a mystery. The jeans became something of a legend—kept close. By 2024—seven years later—we finally accepted the truth: the mystery might never be solved. But we knew what had to be done. We reached out to a dear friend. That friend—since day one—was Kaihara. If anyone could reverse-engineer this denim, it was them. They took on the challenge, analyzing the yarns, slub, color balance, density, and every irregularity with precision. The result? Mystery Selvage—a faithful recreation of one of the most elusive Nudie Jeans we’ve ever come across. Now available in Tuff Tony, our closest modern relative to Loose Frank. And while it may not be quite as rare, it is very limited to only 145 pieces. Enjoy the process of wearing them in—we hope to see a few come back through Re:Use in 16 years or so. Let’s create tomorrow’s vintage.

product_description: Regular-fit jeans with a straight leg in a deep blackish indigo stretch selvage. Crafted from 13 oz greencast stretch selvage denim from Kaihara Mills, Japan, these jeans feature a rare black selvage ID, paired with black thread and dull black trims for a quietly bold, unified look. The slubby, ring-spun yarns give the fabric a rigid-like texture, with pronounced vertical warp slub adding depth and character from the first wear. Dyed in indigo over a sulphur-black base, the warp delivers the denim’s deep, blackish indigo tone, while the greyish-beige weft softens it just enough for subtle complexity. As the jeans break in, high-contrast wear and a distinctive greencast hue emerge, gradually revealing a rarely seen Nudie Jeans expression. Over time, the interplay between fabric, stitching, and trims intensifies—becoming more uniquely unapologetic.

product_name: Steady Eddie II Dry Black Selvage
product_description: Regular-fit jeans with a tapered leg crafted from our all-time favorite Japanese black selvedge denim. This 14.5 oz. denim boasts a tight weave density and generous starch application, ensuring remarkable rigidity. But what truly distinguishes it is its exceptional aging property, a testament to Kurabo Mills' blend of heritage and innovative denim craftsmanship. This denim stands out due to its unique construction: the warp, visible predominantly on the fabric's face, is yarn-dyed black, allowing it to gradually fade with wear and washes. Conversely, the weft, visible on predominantly on the fabric's back, is dyed with a 'stay black' dye, retaining its deep black hue without fading. Although this might seem unconventional for fading denim, it yields intriguing contrasts, emphasizing the warp-weft intersections. This clever construction promises more pronounced contrasting fades compared to regular dry black denim. Beyond its unique yarn combination, the denim's tight weave, stiff texture, pronounced twill lines and slightly coarse yarns create an exquisite surface structure and marbling effects with wear and washes. For optimal results, we recommend wearing our dry denim regularly for at least six months before the first wash. While entirely optional, this period encourages striking fades.

denim_cat: 13.5 oz. Japanese comfort stretch selvedge denim
product_description: Slim fit jeans with a slim leg in a remarkable Japanese stretch selvedge denim meticulously woven by the legendary Kaihara Mills in Japan. This orange-line selvedge, exclusively woven for us, boasts a rigid-like texture with pronounced coarseness and a distinct 3-dimensional feel, accentuated by clear twill lines. The ring-spun warp and weft yarns offer moderate slubbiness, promising a distinct salt-and-pepper texture as the denim ages, and revealing clear twill lines in areas prone to abrasion. Achieved through 20 rounds of dip-dyeing in pure indigo, Kaihara's darkest and most saturated hue graces the warp yarns, the most important feature of the Dry Japan Selvage. This ensures that the warp yarns ages into clear, redcast, blue hues over time while maintaining contrast and depth in areas untouched by wear. Dry Japan Selvage has demonstrated exceptional aging qualities over the years, earning its place as one of our most cherished Japanese selvedge denims, now available in Solid Ollie. Enjoy the journey! We recommend a minimum of 6 months of daily wear before the first wash, although longer wear enhances the unique aging process. While it may seem lengthy, it's a suggestion; your jeans will acquire character with love and wear.

product_description: We are thrilled to introduce our high-waist, regular straight fit, Rad Rufus in the extraordinary Dry Ace Selvage. This remarkable fabric, weighing 14.2 oz showcase a rustic texture and a deep, redcast indigo hue, is a true masterpiece. Crafted by the renowned Shinya Mills in Okayama, Japan, it exemplifies the art of perfecting selvedge denim imperfections. The story behind Dry Ace Selvage is as fascinating as the fabric itself. It all began with a vintage, unbranded pair of jeans believed to date back to the 1950s, discovered in a thrift store somewhere in the USA. These jeans displayed wear and significant fading, and most importantly an incredible texture. They made their way to Japan, where denim from the jeans was meticulously reverse-engineered in exhaustive detail. The art of reverse engineering denim is a hallmark of the artisanal Japanese denim industry. Dry Ace Selvage is a remarkable example of this craft, as well as a testament to the unique collaborative approach sometimes employed for premium Japanese denim. The rather extreme slub yarns are spun by Yamachu Mengyo Co. Ltd. in Osaka. The warp yarns undergo an impressive 20 dips in pure indigo dye, resulting in a deeply saturated surface with a notably substantial undyed core, courtesy of Kaihara Co. Ltd. in Hiroshima. Finally, the fabric is masterfully woven under low tension on vintage shuttle looms by Shinya Mills in Okayama. The guiding principle here is "do what you do best," and the final product beautifully reflects the craftsmanship at each stage of the denim's creation. Ace's distinctive character arises from its thick and highly irregular yarns, generous use of indigo, and low-tension weaving. These factors contribute to its appealingly rugged aging property. Over time, the dye rapidly abrades from the surface of the yarns, emphasizing their slub, particularly in the vertical direction. When, or if, the jeans are washed, the yarns slightly rearrange, reducing the vertical slub and transitioning to a rustic crosshatch texture with a coarse salt 'n pepper effect. Notably, the heavily saturated surface of the indigo yarns entails an extraordinary evolution. Washing initially causes the dye to bleed onto the white weft, increasing the depth of color. With successive washes, the indigo gradually fades into brighter brilliant blue hues while deeper tones are preserved at seams and folds. Due to its unparalleled aging property, Ace unquestionably stands as one of the finest Japanese selvedge denims we have ever encountered. We are therefore very proud to offer it in Rad Rufus.

product_description: Regular fit jeans with a straight leg made in organic denim that stays black. Our Everblack fabric is made to maintain its color for as long as possible, withstanding 40, or more washes without any significant loss of color. To optimize the denim for the fit, these jeans have been rinsed, making the jeans stretchy and soft. If you are looking for a pair of pitch-black jeans that do not fade this is the way to go. Keen on knowing what differentiates Everblack from other black denim? Normally, when denim is dyed part of the dye will absorb in the cotton fibers while the rest remains bound only to the surface of the yarns. This is what gives denim its aging property that in many cases is sought after. However, when it comes to black jeans this is not always the case which is why we developed the Everblack denim. The technical difference between Everblack and regular black denim is that it utilizes a method and type of dyestuff that allows improved absorption as well as a stronger fixation. This results in denim with a significantly greater colorfastness, which does not fade with wearing and washing

product_description: Regular-fit jeans with a mid waist and straight leg. This fabric was developed exclusively for Nudie Jeans back in 2022. Crafted and brought to life by the Japanese denim powerhouse Kurabo Denim. At 13.5 oz. This mid-weight denim hits a sweet spot between weight and robust feel. The fabric carries a hand that is more textured than smooth while still offering great comfort. While it's barely visible in the dry state, this denim features a green cast that'll show its full potential only when faded and broken in. The fabric composition features long slub, ring-spun yarns for both warp and weft, which creates an evident crosshatch texture. A revival of a great fabric. Limited to 150 pieces. Breaking in dry denim is a metamorphosis. The interplay between the garment and the user is a spectacular transformation happening slowly in front of your eyes. Over time, with wearing and washing, the indigo fades, exposing the fabric's texture, and eventually unveiling an intricate span of hues. This creates a unique, one-off version of the jeans.

product_description: The Tuff Tony Dry Sunburns stands out with its gradient back pocket embroidery, shifting from deep orange to pale yellow, with our own Nudie Jeans Orange at the center. Paired with a special graphic Jacron patch, it subtly plays with our identity and iconography. Beneath it all lies a proprietary 14.2 oz denim, developed exclusively for Nudie Jeans. Woven with Regenerative Organic Cotton, it revives the raw, textured character of a pair of ‘90s jeans we once stumbled upon. Though undeniably "modern," these jeans are rooted in a transitional era—a time when vintage ring-spun textures resurfaced, and select denim mills strayed from pure efficiency in pursuit of true vintage aesthetics. A defining moment—just before Nudie Jeans was born. Regenerative organic cotton meets all the standards of organic cotton, avoiding synthetic chemicals and GMOs, but goes further by restoring ecosystems, improving soil health, and boosting biodiversity. It considers the whole system – land, water, people, and long-term resilience.

product_description: This right here is special to us, the 13.75 Kaihara Dry Selvage. It left us in awe back in 2001, and thanks to its incredible aging property, it still does to this day. It is the first selvedge denim we ever used, and it is one reason Nudie Jeans exists. This is our heritage. You see, it was when one of our founders, Maria, got her eyes on this particular denim that what is today Nudie Jeans started to take form. And it's no wonder as it is exceptionally appealing - it has a deep indigo warp, unbleached, vintage-inspired ecru weft, tight construction, dry hand, and starchy crispiness. Yet, the greatest part of the denim is its aging property - how it evolves with wearing. To understand it, let us get a bit technical. The warp is first dyed in a dark sulfur dye and subsequently in indigo. This dyeing technique gives the Dry Selvage the deep and muted indigo blue hue that looks so utterly beautiful, accompanied by our orange thread. It is also what gives the denim its intriguing nuances as it ages. It is what we call a greencast denim, denim that has a slight greenish-blue hue that becomes more evident with wearing. With years of wear and some washes - as the dark denim turns lighter in tone - the greencast starts to lean towards a redcast - clear blue - hue. This is why it is so intriguingly beautiful and has remained in our collection since the start of Nudie Jeans back in 2001. We are very proud to offer Gritty Jackson Dry Selvage - our hats off for Kaihara's denim and indigo artisans, forever. We recommend 6 months of everyday wear before the first wash, if not longer. This way, you will end up with a beautiful pair of worn-in jeans. It sure is a long time; however, it's only a recommendation. As long as you give them love, the result will be great.

product_description: Slim-fit jeans with a slim leg crafted from pitch-black, "black overdyed black denim", the Dry Pure Black offers a soft hand feel and a deep black hue that gradually fades into smooth, gray tones. The ring-spun denim showcases a uniform texture with a hint of vertical streakiness. Over time, this streakiness becomes more pronounced with wear, and subtle marble effects emerge through washing, creating a rich, evolving texture marked by muted black and gray tones. The greatest part of these jeans is not what they look like new, but rather the complete opposite - how they will look tomorrow, and the many days to come. Enjoy aging, it is worth the trouble.

product_description: Lean Dean Dry Sunburns stands out with its gradient back pocket embroidery, shifting from deep orange to pale yellow, with our own Nudie Jeans Orange at the center. Paired with a special graphic Jacron patch, it subtly plays with our identity and iconography. Underneath it all is a 13 oz denim, dyed with an indigo top over a sulfur-dyed bottom, creating a deep, almost inky tone that holds its intensity even as it fades. This fabric doesn’t just age—it evolves, setting the stage for wear that reveals stark contrasts and layers of character. Its rigid-like texture, with a balance of yarn slub and salt-and-pepper grain, carries an undeniable rawness. The clear twill lines cut through the texture, adding structure, while the stretch gives just enough comfort without ever dulling the ruggedness. A long-time favorite of ours, this denim delivers a level of character unlike any other stretch fabric. It’s built for the long haul, destined to become tomorrow’s vintage.

product_description: Regular fit jeans with a straight leg, made from 13 oz. rigid Turkish selvage denim crafted from organic and recycled cotton, dyed with pure natural indigo. The fabric features a neat all-white selvage ID and a gently slubby texture, with pronounced vertical twill lines. The deep, natural indigo tone gives these jeans a classic, well-balanced look. Our signature orange thread provides a bold contrast, while the navy back pocket embroidery becomes more prominent as the denim ages. With wear, these selvage jeans develop a unique, worn-in character that is unique to the wearer. Over time, these jeans will evolve a distinct patina, slowly turning vintage.

product_name: Steady Eddie II Dry Ace Selvage
product_description: Jeans made in a beautiful, sturdy, 14.2 oz, redcast selvedge denim woven by Shinya Mills in Okayama, Japan. The warp yarns have been dip dyed 20 times in pure indigo, giving the jeans its dark, pure indigo, redcast tone. The naturally slubby yarns, woven under low-tension have produced the perfectly chunky and uneven surface structure. Making this fabric hit the very spot where imperfection turns to perfection - a true denim masterpiece. If that was not enough this denim fabric has a history; it is a recreation of a denim used for a pair of jeans from the 1950s. The jeans were found at a vintage market, worn-in and utterly beautiful. In awe of the look, the good people at Shinya took it upon themselves to replicate the denim fabric. And boy are we happy they did! These Steady Eddie II's are, much thanks to the fabric, simply ace. If you are looking for a 'top of the line' Japanese selvedge that develops a marbling, salt 'n pepper structure like the best vintage denim did, then this is by all means it. We are super excited to be able to offer the Steady Eddie II Dry Ace Selvage. Let the break-in begin!

product_description: Slim-fit jeans with a straight leg in a remarkable Japanese stretch selvedge denim meticulously woven by the legendary Kaihara Mills in Japan. This orange-line selvedge, exclusively woven for us, boasts a rigid-like texture with pronounced coarseness and a distinct 3-dimensional feel, accentuated by clear twill lines. The ring-spun warp and weft yarns offer moderate slubbiness, promising a distinct salt-and-pepper texture as the denim ages, and revealing clear twill lines in areas prone to abrasion. Achieved through 20 rounds of dip-dyeing in pure indigo, Kaihara's darkest and most saturated hue graces the warp yarns, the most important feature of the Dry Japan Selvage. This ensures that the warp yarns age into clear, redcast, blue hues over time while maintaining contrast and depth in areas untouched by wear. Dry Japan Selvage has demonstrated exceptional aging qualities over the years, earning its place as one of our most cherished Japanese selvedge denims, now available in Grim Tim. Enjoy the journey! We recommend a minimum of 6 months of daily wear before the first wash, although longer wear enhances the unique aging process. While it may seem lengthy, it's a suggestion; your jeans will acquire character with love and wear.

product_description: Wide-legged, loose-fit jeans crafted from our all-time favorite Japanese black selvedge denim. This 14.5 oz. denim boasts a tight weave density and generous starch application, ensuring remarkable rigidity. But what truly distinguishes it is its exceptional aging property, a testament to Kurabo Mills' blend of heritage and innovative denim craftsmanship. This denim stands out due to its unique construction: the warp, visible predominantly on the fabric's face, is yarn-dyed black, allowing it to gradually fade with wear and washes. Conversely, the weft, visible predominantly on the fabric's back, is dyed with a 'stay black' dye, retaining its deep black hue without fading. Although this might seem unconventional for fading denim, it yields intriguing contrasts, emphasizing the warp-weft intersections. This clever construction promises more pronounced contrasting fades compared to regular dry black denim. Beyond its unique yarn combination, the denim's tight weave, stiff texture, pronounced twill lines and slightly coarse yarns create an exquisite surface structure and marbling effects with wear and washes. For optimal results, we recommend wearing our dry denim regularly for at least six months before the first wash. While entirely optional, this period encourages striking fades.

product_description: High-waist, regular-fit jeans with a straight leg, made from 13 oz. rigid Turkish selvage denim crafted from organic and recycled cotton, dyed with pure natural indigo. The fabric features a neat all-white selvage ID and a gently slubby texture, with pronounced vertical twill lines. The deep, natural indigo tone gives these jeans a classic, well-balanced look. Our signature orange thread provides a bold contrast, while the navy back pocket embroidery becomes more prominent as the denim ages. With wear, these selvage jeans develop a unique, worn-in character that is unique to the wearer. For the best results, we recommend at least 6 months of wear before the first wash, though earlier washing is also fine. For care, hand wash or use a gentle machine cycle. Over time, these jeans will evolve a distinct patina, slowly turning vintage.

product_description: Mid waist regular-fit jean with a flared leg crafted in the bold and unapologetic "Old News" wash. With a deeply saturated base, strong highlights, and a greenish tint that dirties it all down, this wash brings out the denim’s rich texture, showing off beautiful vertical slubbiness and subtle marble effects. Old News strikes a perfect balance between raw authenticity and deliberate exaggeration—blurring the line just enough. However you see it, one thing’s for sure—it’s beautifully gruesome in all the right ways. Regenerative organic cotton meets all the standards of organic cotton, avoiding synthetic chemicals and GMOs, but goes further by restoring ecosystems, improving soil health, and boosting biodiversity. It considers the whole system – land, water, people, and long-term resilience.

product_description: This 14.15 oz. dry denim greatness bears the resemblance of an old, '50s style, denim. True to history it is constructed of ring-spun warp and weft yarns with irregular thickness, as was often the case back in the day on workwear fabrics. Also true to its heritage is the dark, but not blackish, indigo warp that is dyed in pure indigo. With wear and wash, the yarns will interact, and you'll reap the rewards that heavier ring-spun denim produces — evident slubs, a pleasing crosshatch as well as a marbling -effect. It's the irregular yarns in both warp and weft that render the crosshatch effect, and the built-in tension formed by the yarns that produce the marble effect after washing. As this denim is dyed in pure indigo, it will age to a clean, blue redcast tone. The greatest part of these jeans is not what they look like new, but rather the complete opposite - how they will look tomorrow, and the many days to come. Enjoy ageing, it is worth the trouble.

product_description: It is with the warmest of welcomes we greet Gritty Jackson Dry Ace Selvage to our range of selvedge jeans. Gritty Jackson is our take on the perhaps most versatile and iconic fit ever - the regular, straight, fit. As such it was inevitable to pair it with the Dry Ace Selvage fabric - one of our absolute favorite selvedge fabrics out there. The Dry Ace Selvage is an utterly beautiful and sturdy 14.2 oz, redcast selvedge denim. It is woven by none other than Shinya Mills in Okayama, Japan. The warp yarns have been dip-dyed 20 times in pure indigo, giving the denim its dark, pure indigo, redcast tone. The naturally slubby yarns, woven under low-tension, has produced the perfectly chunky and uneven surface structure, making this fabric hit the very spot where imperfection turns into perfection. And that is by no means without cause, Shinya Mills, being outstandingly great at denim, recreated the Dry Ace Selvage fabric by analyzing the denim of a pair of worn-in jeans from the 50s. Dig that, recreating a dry denim from a washed, worn, and torn pair of jeans – that is some reverse engineering right there! Now, this is just the starting point from which the Gritty Jackson Dry Ace Selvage will evolve from. See, we appreciate Dry Ace in its dry state - but in its worn-in - washed or unwashed - state it is marvelously good looking. Due to its redcast tone it evolves to a clear blue hue, its uneven structure generates a salt 'n pepper structure, and where abrasions get most predominant the wear will generate clear twill lines. Needless to say, this denim justifies its name. So without further ado - behold the almighty Gritty Jackson Dry Ace Selvage!

product_description: We are thrilled to introduce Steady Eddie II in the Gravity Selvage – an exquisite 18 oz. heavyweight selvedge denim from the renowned Kaihara Mills in Japan. This heavyweight selvedge holds a special place in our hearts. Initially unveiled in 2015 under the name "Steady Eddie Dry Heavy Japan Selvage," it was a limited edition, with only 1000 pairs in existence, rendering it a rare gem. However, every time we come across a worn pair of these jeans, they never fail to leave us spellbound. The visual ruggedness of the denim first captures the eye, and upon picking them up, the weight and tactility immediately confirm that this denim is truly extraordinary. So extraordinary that, almost a decade later, we approached Kaihara to weave this exceptional selvedge once more – a challenging feat realized on vintage shuttle looms. As the master denim craftsmen that Kaihara is, we were confident they would accept the challenge, and we are sincerely humbled that they did. Woven from coarse ringspun yarns at a high density, the denim is stiff, thick, and heavy, providing a distinctive visual and tactile experience in its dry state. Dyed to a deep, dark shade of pure indigo, the coarseness is further emphasized with wear and washing, creating striking highs and lows as the indigo gracefully chips away. This denim serves as a testament to Kaihara's expert skills in both selvedge weaving and indigo dyeing, affirming their mastery of the art of denim.

denim_cat: 12.75 oz. turkish comfort stretch selvedge denim
product_description: Without a doubt a favorite of ours - the almighty Lean Dean in a crispy dry black comfort stretch selvedge denim. Weighing in at 12.75 oz. the denim is nicely coupled with Dean's slim fit. The starch in the denim gives it its crispy hand and, most importantly, generates pronounced creases on the jeans when worn - allowing for some contrasting moustaches and honeycombs. The denim itself is woven on old school shuttle looms with yarns that have been yarn dyed. The shuttle looms interlace the yarns into the selvedge denim and the yarn dying keeps the core of the yarns white allowing the weave to fade beautifully - a slow process that reflects how denim was made back in the day. The finished denim has a clear twill structure which is nicely balanced by a slight slub character which with wear and washing will get more pronounced in a perfectly imperfect way. As with all our dry denim we recommend at least 6 months of daily wear before the first wash to achieve contrasting fades. This is of course just a recommendation.

product_description: Mid-waist, regular-fit jeans with flared leg crafted from 14.2 oz. dry denim in a deep blue hue, accentuated by Nudie Jeans' signature orange stitching. As the jeans age with wear and washing, the yarns will interact, revealing the distinctive characteristics of this ring-spun denim—prominent slubs, a pleasing crosshatch pattern, and a marbled effect. The irregular yarns in both the warp and weft create the crosshatch, while the built-in tension produces the marble-like texture after washing. Dyed in pure indigo, the denim will develop a rich blue-redcast tone over time. The greatest part of these jeans is not what they look like new, but rather the complete opposite - how they will look tomorrow, and the many days to come. Enjoy aging; it is worth the trouble.
